Massive online retailers Temu and Shein raised prices on their products after the Trump administration imposed new tariffs on China and closed a loophole that allowed duty-free shipment of low-cost Chinese goods to U.S. households."},{"url":"https://abc7.com/post/china-founded-commerce-sites-temu-shein-say-raising-prices-due-tariffs/16196680/","title":"China-founded e-commerce sites Temu and Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs - ABC7 Los Angeles","content":"Temu, which is owned by the Chinese e-commerce company PDD Holdings, and Shein, which is now based in Singapore, said in separate but nearly identical notices that their operating expenses have gone up "due to recent changes in global trade rules and tariffs."
Both companies said they would be making "price adjustments" starting April 25, although neither provided details about the size of the increases.
Trump signed an executive order this month to eliminate the "de minimis provision" for goods from China and Hong Kong starting May 2, when they will be subject to the 145% import tax.
Last year, the companies were among the largest advertising spenders on social media platforms, but they've both slashed that spending in recent weeks, according to data analytics provider Sensor Tower.
That could be bad news for the platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Snap, X and TikTok that rely on advertising.
In November, American e-commerce giant Amazon launched a low-cost online storefront featuring electronics, apparel and other products priced at under $20.
Many of the electronics, apparel and other products on the storefront Wednesday resembled the types of items typically found on Shein and Temu."},{"url":"https://www.livenowfox.com/news/temu-shein-raising-us-prices-china-tariffs","title":"Temu and Shein, both founded in China, raising US prices due to tariffs | LiveNOW from FOX","content":"China announced Friday that it will raise tariffs on U.S. goods from 84% to 125% — the latest salvo in an escalating trade war between the world’s two largest economies that has rattled markets and raised fears of a global slowdown.
President Donald Trump paused import taxes this week for other countries, he raised tariffs on China and they now total 145%.
Temu and Shein, both China-founded online companies, plan to raise prices for U.S. customers starting next week amid President Donald Trump’s tariff on goods shipped from China.
20, 2025, in London, England."},{"url":"https://abcnews.com/Business/shein-temu-price-increases-set-effect/story?id=121155117","title":"Shein and Temu set to raise prices in response to tariffs - ABC News","content":"China-based online retailers Shein and Temu are set to hike prices for U.S. customers on Friday as they weather costs imposed by President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
Both firms set a date of April 25 on which the price hike would take effect.
Shein did not immediately respond to ABC News' request for comment about the exact timing and level of price increases."},{"url":"https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/temu-shein-raising-prices-1.7512710","title":"Fast fashion e-commerce sites Temu and Shein say they're raising prices because of tariffs | CBC News","content":"Both of the China-founded sites say U.S. customers will see higher prices starting next week, and industry data shows they're pulling back on U.S. advertising, too.
President Donald Trump plans to remove the de minimis exemption, which excludes packages worth less than $800 US from tariffs.
Temu's daily average U.S. ad spend on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Snap, X and YouTube declined a collective average of 31 per cent in the two weeks from March 31 to April 13, compared with the previous 30 days, estimated Sensor Tower, which tracks such spending."}]}
